name: io.github.Muvon/octofs
description: Standalone MCP filesystem tools server — view, edit, shell, workdir.

tools.name: view
tools.description: Read files, view directories, and search file content. Unified read-only tool.

path.description: A single file or directory path (e.g. "src/main.rs"). To view multiple files, make separate view calls — they run in parallel.

start.description: First line to show (inclusive): integer line number (negative counts from the end, -1 = last line) or a string hash in hash mode. Omit to start at line 1.

end.description: Last line to show (inclusive): integer line number or string hash. Omit to read to end of file. Omit BOTH start and end to view the whole file.

pattern.description: Filename glob filter for directory listing (e.g. '*.rs'). Only used when path is a directory.

content.description: Content search string. Literal substring by default; set `regex: true` for a regex. Works when path is a directory or a single file.

max_depth.description: Maximum directory traversal depth (default: no limit). Only used when path is a directory.

include_hidden.description: Include hidden files/directories starting with '.' (default: false).

regex.description: When true, `content` is a Rust regex (case-insensitive via `(?i)`) instead of a literal substring.

context.description: Context lines around content search matches (default: 0).

tools.name: text_editor
tools.description: Perform text editing operations on files: create new files, replace exact string matches, or undo the last edit.

command.description: The operation to perform: create, str_replace, undo_edit

path.description: Path to the file to operate on.

old_text.description: Text to find and replace (must match exactly) — for str_replace command.

tools.name: batch_edit
tools.description: Perform multiple insert/replace operations on a single file atomically, using original line numbers. Returns a diff of all changes.

path.description: Path to the file to edit.

operations.description: Array of insert/replace operations. All line targets reference the original file content.

operation.description: Type of operation: 'insert' (after a line) or 'replace' (a line range)

start.description: Start line (integer) or hash (string). insert: anchor — 0 (file start), -1 (after last line), N (after line N). replace: first line of the range.

end.description: Last line of the range to replace (inclusive). Omit for a single-line replace (defaults to start). Ignored for insert.

content.description: Content to insert or replace with.

tools.name: extract_lines
tools.description: Copy lines from a source file and append them into a target file. Source is not modified.

from_path.description: Path to the source file to extract lines from.

from_start.description: First line to copy (inclusive): integer line number or string hash.

from_end.description: Last line to copy (inclusive): integer line number or string hash. Omit to copy a single line (defaults to from_start).

append_path.description: Path to the target file where extracted lines will be appended (auto-created if doesn't exist).

append_line.description: Where to append: 0 = beginning, -1 = end, N = after line N (integer), or a string hash in hash mode.

tools.name: shell
tools.description: Execute a command in the shell. Returns stdout+stderr combined, with success/failure indication. Each command runs in its own process — state does not persist between calls.

command.description: The shell command to execute (runs from current working directory).

background.description: Run command in background and return PID instead of waiting for completion.

tools.name: workdir
tools.description: Get or set the working directory used by all MCP tools (shell, text_editor, etc.). Changes apply to the current thread only.

path.description: Optional path to set as new working directory. Can be absolute or relative to current working directory.

reset.description: If true, reset to original project directory (ignores 'path' parameter).
